annotations.label: Abelian group

anchor.snippet: must satisfy four requirements known as the abelian group axioms

annotations.note: `CommGroup` (and additively `AddCommGroup`) is exactly a group with a commutative operation.

annotations.label: Non-abelian group

anchor.snippet: A group in which the group operation is not commutative is called

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated 'non-abelian' predicate; it is just the negation of `IsMulCommutative`/`CommGroup`.

annotations.label: Cayley table

anchor.snippet: known as a Cayley table

annotations.note: Cayley/multiplication tables are not formalized as a structure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian iff Cayley table symmetric

anchor.snippet: The group is abelian if and only if this table is symmetric about the main diagonal

annotations.note: No Cayley-table symmetry characterization exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trivial group is abelian

anchor.snippet: The group consisting of only the identity element is abelian

annotations.note: `PUnit.commGroup` gives the trivial (one-element) group a `CommGroup` instance.

annotations.label: Integers under addition

anchor.snippet: the operation + combines any two integers to form a third integer

annotations.note: `Int.instAddCommGroup` is the additive abelian group structure on ℤ.

annotations.label: Every cyclic group is abelian

anchor.snippet: is abelian, because if

annotations.note: `IsCyclic.commGroup` / `IsCyclic.isMulCommutative` show any cyclic group is commutative.

annotations.label: Ring is abelian group under addition

anchor.snippet: Every ring is an abelian group with respect to its addition operation

annotations.note: `NonUnitalNonAssocRing` (hence every `Ring`) extends `AddCommGroup`.

annotations.label: Units form abelian multiplicative group

anchor.snippet: the invertible elements, or units , form an abelian multiplicative group

annotations.note: `instCommGroupUnits` makes the units of a `CommMonoid` a `CommGroup`.

annotations.label: Real numbers as abelian groups

anchor.snippet: the real numbers are an abelian group under addition

annotations.note: ℝ gets an `AddCommGroup` instance from its field structure in `Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic`.

annotations.label: Subgroups of abelian groups are normal

anchor.snippet: Every subgroup of an abelian group is normal

annotations.note: `normal_of_isMulCommutative` is an instance making every subgroup of a commutative group normal.

annotations.label: Closure under subgroups, quotients, direct sums

anchor.snippet: Subgroups, quotients, and direct sums of abelian groups are again abelian

annotations.note: `Subgroup.toCommGroup` (with analogous `CommGroup` instances on quotients and products) shows abelianness is preserved.

annotations.label: Finite simple abelian groups are cyclic of prime order

anchor.snippet: The finite simple abelian groups are exactly the cyclic groups of prime order

annotations.note: `CommGroup.is_simple_iff_prime_card` plus `IsSimpleGroup.isCyclic` give that simple abelian groups are exactly prime-order cyclic groups.

annotations.label: Abelian groups equal Z-modules

anchor.snippet: The concepts of abelian group and

annotations.note: The forgetful functor `ModuleCat ℤ → AddCommGrp` is an equivalence, identifying abelian groups with ℤ-modules.

annotations.label: Matrices and rotation matrices

anchor.snippet: do not form an abelian group under multiplication because matrix multiplication is generally not commutative

annotations.note: Non-commutativity of matrix multiplication is not stated as a named theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian group as Z-module

anchor.snippet: becomes a module over the ring

annotations.note: `AddCommGroup.toIntModule` equips every additive abelian group with a canonical ℤ-module structure.

annotations.label: Structure of finitely generated abelian groups

anchor.snippet: an abelian group splits as a direct sum of a torsion group and a free abelian group

annotations.note: `equiv_free_prod_directSum_zmod` decomposes a f.g. abelian group as a free part times a direct sum of prime-power cyclic groups.

annotations.label: Sum of homomorphisms is a homomorphism

anchor.snippet: is again a homomorphism

annotations.note: `AddMonoidHom.add` (multiplicatively `MonoidHom.mul`) defines the pointwise sum of homomorphisms into a commutative target as a homomorphism.

annotations.label: Rank of an abelian group

anchor.snippet: It is defined as the maximal cardinality of a set of linearly independent

annotations.note: `Module.rank ℤ G` is the supremum of cardinalities of linearly independent families, i.e. the torsion-free rank, generalized to modules.

annotations.label: Rank zero iff torsion

anchor.snippet: Finite abelian groups and torsion groups have rank zero

annotations.note: `rank_eq_zero_iff_isTorsion` states rank 0 ↔ torsion for modules over a domain, which specializes to ℤ-modules.

annotations.label: Center of a group

anchor.snippet: is the set of elements that commute with every element

annotations.note: `Subgroup.center` is the subgroup of elements commuting with every element.

annotations.label: Abelian iff equal to its center

anchor.snippet: is abelian if and only if it is equal to its center

annotations.note: `center_eq_top_iff` states `center G = ⊤ ↔ IsMulCommutative G`.

annotations.label: Cyclic quotient by center implies abelian

anchor.snippet: of a group by its center is cyclic then

annotations.note: `commutative_of_cyclic_center_quotient` / `commGroupOfCyclicCenterQuotient` prove a group is commutative when its quotient by the center is cyclic.

annotations.label: Finite abelian group as direct sum of prime-power cyclic groups

anchor.snippet: an arbitrary finite abelian group is isomorphic to a direct sum of finite cyclic groups of prime power order

annotations.note: `equiv_directSum_zmod_of_finite` gives the direct-sum-of-prime-power-cyclic decomposition of any finite abelian group.

annotations.label: Group of prime order is cyclic

anchor.snippet: Any group of prime order is isomorphic to a cyclic group and therefore abelian

annotations.note: `isCyclic_of_prime_card` shows any group of prime cardinality is cyclic (hence abelian).

annotations.label: Group of order p-squared is abelian

anchor.snippet: Any group whose order is a square of a prime number is also abelian

annotations.note: `commutative_of_card_eq_prime_sq` (and `commGroupOfCardEqPrimeSq`) prove groups of order p² are commutative.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of finite abelian groups

anchor.snippet: can be expressed as the direct sum of cyclic subgroups of prime

annotations.note: `equiv_directSum_zmod_of_finite` is the fundamental theorem expressing finite abelian groups as direct sums of prime-power cyclic groups.

annotations.label: Cyclic group splits iff orders coprime

anchor.snippet: are coprime

annotations.note: `ZMod.chineseRemainder` gives `ZMod (m*n) ≃+* ZMod m × ZMod n` when m,n are coprime (the substantive direction).

annotations.label: Decomposition of order 15 group

anchor.snippet: can be expressed as the direct sum of two cyclic subgroups of order 3 and 5

annotations.note: The specific Z/15 ≅ Z/3 × Z/5 example is not a named decl but is an immediate instance of `ZMod.chineseRemainder`.

annotations.label: Abelian groups of order 8

anchor.snippet: every abelian group of order 8 is isomorphic to either

annotations.note: There is no enumerated classification of order-8 abelian groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Automorphism group splits over coprime summands
annotations.note: Mathlib does not have Aut(G×H) ≅ Aut(G)×Aut(H) for coprime-order summands.

anchor.snippet: of subgroups of coprime order

annotations.label: Automorphisms as invertible linear transformations

anchor.snippet: are therefore given by the invertible linear transformations

annotations.note: The identification of automorphisms of an elementary abelian p-group with GLₙ(𝔽ₚ) is not formalized (only the cyclic case `MulAut G ≃* (ZMod _)ˣ` exists).

annotations.label: Finitely generated abelian group

anchor.snippet: if it contains a finite set of elements

annotations.note: `AddGroup.FG` (multiplicatively `Group.FG`) is the predicate that a group is finitely generated.

annotations.label: Unique homomorphism from free abelian group

anchor.snippet: There is a unique group homomorphism

annotations.note: `FreeAbelianGroup.lift` is the universal-property equivalence giving the unique homomorphism extending a function on generators.

annotations.label: Homomorphism surjective with f.g. kernel

anchor.snippet: This homomorphism is surjective

annotations.note: The presentation statement (surjection from ℤⁿ with finitely generated kernel) is not packaged as a Mathlib result.

annotations.label: Equivalence with integer matrices

anchor.snippet: is totally equivalent with the study of integer matrices

annotations.note: The reduction of f.g. abelian groups to integer matrices is not stated as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smith normal form

anchor.snippet: The Smith normal form of M is a matrix

annotations.note: `Module.Basis.SmithNormalForm` and `Submodule.smithNormalForm` formalize Smith normal form for submodules over a PID.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of finitely generated abelian groups

anchor.snippet: the finitely generated abelian group A is the direct sum

annotations.note: `equiv_free_prod_directSum_zmod` is the fundamental theorem for finitely generated abelian groups.

annotations.label: Infinite cyclic group

anchor.snippet: The simplest infinite abelian group is the infinite cyclic group

annotations.note: The instance `IsAddCyclic ℤ` realizes ℤ as the infinite cyclic group.

annotations.label: Decomposition of finitely generated abelian groups

anchor.snippet: and a finite abelian group, which in turn is decomposable

annotations.note: `equiv_free_prod_directSum_zmod` decomposes a f.g. abelian group into ℤⁿ times a finite abelian part.

annotations.label: Divisible group

anchor.snippet: Divisible groups

annotations.note: `DivisibleBy A α` formalizes divisibility of an additive group/monoid.

annotations.label: Classification of divisible groups

anchor.snippet: Every divisible group is isomorphic to a direct sum

annotations.note: The structure theorem expressing divisible groups as sums of ℚ's and Prüfer groups is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Divisible subgroup is a direct summand
annotations.note: Mathlib proves divisible groups are injective objects (`injective_of_divisible`), but the resulting direct-summand statement is not stated.

anchor.snippet: admits a direct complement

annotations.label: Injective abelian groups are divisible (Baer)

anchor.snippet: every injective abelian group is divisible

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the converse (divisible ⟹ injective); the stated direction injective ⟹ divisible is not present.

annotations.label: Reduced abelian group

anchor.snippet: An abelian group without non-zero divisible subgroups is called reduced

annotations.note: There is no definition of a reduced abelian group in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Periodic (torsion) group

anchor.snippet: if every element has finite order

annotations.note: `Monoid.IsTorsion` / `AddMonoid.IsTorsion` is the predicate that every element has finite order.

annotations.label: Direct sum of finite cyclic groups is periodic

anchor.snippet: A direct sum of finite cyclic groups is periodic

annotations.note: The torsion predicate and torsion-preservation lemmas exist, but no single decl states a direct sum of finite cyclic groups is torsion.

annotations.label: Prüfer theorems

anchor.snippet: The first and second Prüfer theorems state that

annotations.note: The Prüfer theorems on countable abelian p-groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ulm classification of countable p-groups

anchor.snippet: completely classified by means of their Ulm invariants

annotations.note: Ulm invariants and the classification of countable p-groups are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Torsion-free abelian group

anchor.snippet: if every non-zero element has infinite order

annotations.note: `Monoid.IsTorsionFree` / `AddMonoid.IsTorsionFree` formalizes torsion-freeness.

annotations.label: Mixed abelian group

anchor.snippet: is neither periodic nor torsion-free is called mixed

annotations.note: There is no 'mixed group' definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quotient by torsion subgroup is torsion-free

anchor.snippet: then the factor group

annotations.note: `Module.IsTorsionFree R (M ⧸ torsion R M)` over a domain specializes (R = ℤ) to the quotient of an abelian group by its torsion being torsion-free.

annotations.label: Rank of an infinite abelian group

anchor.snippet: the cardinality of the maximal linearly independent subset

annotations.note: `Module.rank ℤ G` is the supremum of cardinalities of linearly independent families, i.e. the torsion-free rank.

annotations.label: Rank 0 groups are periodic

anchor.snippet: Abelian groups of rank 0 are precisely the periodic groups

annotations.note: `rank_eq_zero_iff_isTorsion` states rank 0 ↔ torsion for modules over a domain, applying to ℤ-modules.

annotations.label: Finite-rank torsion-free as subgroup of Q^n

anchor.snippet: a torsion-free abelian group of finite rank

annotations.note: The embedding of finite-rank torsion-free abelian groups into ℚⁿ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Additive group of a ring is abelian

anchor.snippet: The additive group of a ring is an abelian group

annotations.note: `NonUnitalNonAssocRing` extends `AddCommGroup`, so every ring's additive group is abelian.

annotations.label: Category of abelian groups is an abelian category

anchor.snippet: the prototype of an abelian category

annotations.note: The `Abelian AddCommGrpCat` instance establishes the category of abelian groups as an abelian category.

annotations.label: First-order theory of abelian groups is decidable

anchor.snippet: the first-order theory of abelian groups, unlike its non-abelian counterpart, is decidable

annotations.note: Decidability of the first-order theory of abelian groups is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Whitehead problem undecidable in ZFC

anchor.snippet: are all Whitehead groups of infinite order also free abelian groups

annotations.note: The Whitehead problem and its ZFC-independence are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Additive Group
annotations.note: Mathlib supports both `AddGroup` and `Group` (multiplicative groups)

anchor.snippet: Additive group
